creator: _jkrow
caption:
This is not Europe 🤯

Short on PTO? You don’t have to fly all the way to Europe for vaca - just take a short flight (or drive!) to Québec, Canada - the most European part of North America.

👉 Lika and save this 4 Day Québec City/Charlevoix itinerary for your next getaway:

 ✈️ AIRPORT: Québec City Jean Lesage International Airport (YQB) in Québec, Canada

 ⏰ TIME NEEDED: 4 full days

 🗓 WHEN TO GO: Visit this winter for a winter wonderland and the largest ice castle in North America 

ITINERARY: 

✈️ Day 1: Fly to Québec City, pick up your rental car and check into your hotel. Then take an ebike tour to explore the city, learn some history, and catch sunset from the Québec-Lévis ferry. Finally, head to dinner. Québec City has a huge foodie scene and there are so many amazing restaurants to choose from!

🧊 Day 2: Take an ice canoeing adventure with @canot.a.glace.experience. Then have lunch and soak in the thermal waters at Strøm Spa Nordique before grabbing a drink at the ice bar at Hotel de Glace (weather permitting)

🌿 Day 3: Drive along the St. Lawrence River to Charlevoix, a region that was carved out by a meteorite impact over 400 million years ago.

🧗‍♀️ Day 4: Go hiking or scale the cliffs along the St. Lawrence River on the via ferrata at Fairmont Manoir. Then relax in the heated outdoor pool at the wellness center and treat yourself to a fancy dinner at Le Saint-Laurent restaurant.

——
✨Follow for more PTO friendly itineraries from the East Coast USA 

#travelguides #quebeccanada

transcription.text: This PTO from the getaway feels just like Europe and is only a two-hour direct flight from New York City. Welcome to Quebec City in Charlevoix in Quebec, Canada, and here's how you can spend four days exploring what these areas have to offer. On day one, arrive in Quebec City and check into your hotel. Get a feel for old Quebec City on a walk or on an e-bike tour before grabbing dinner at any of the amazing restaurants nearby. Start day two with an adventure tour like ice canoeing on the St. Lawrence River or tour the world's largest ice hotel, and then treat yourself to ultimate relaxation at St. Spadnordy. On days three and four, hop in the car and drive two hours to Charlevoix, a region known for its mountains, food, and gorgeous scenery. Book a stay at a cozy local inn like La Marmite or splurge on a stay at the Fairmont Manoir. While you're there, go for a hike or scale the cliffs on the Spia Ferrata and indulge at incredible restaurants like Le Saint Laurent to end your trip.
